title: Policy
description: A declarative permission rule the policy engine evaluates against a request. Policies are sorted by priority (higher wins) and the first matching policy decides. The recommended baseline is a default-deny policy at priority 0; all permits are explicit rules at higher priority. Structure is OPA-shaped but expressed as data so the engine can be a small Python evaluator rather than a daemon.

id.description: Policy identifier in kebab-case. Example: agent-tool-permissions-default-deny.

applies_to.description: Scope filter. Each list either enumerates ids or contains the single wildcard string '*'.

roles.description: Role ids in scope, or ['*'] for all roles.

tools.description: Tool ids in scope, or ['*'] for all tools.

actions.description: Action names in scope (free-form, often matching forbidden_actions vocabulary), or ['*'] for all actions.

decision.description: Outcome when the policy matches. require_approval routes the request to the approval queue rather than allowing or denying outright.

conditions.description: Additional predicates that all must hold for the policy to match. Empty array means the applies_to filter is sufficient.

field.description: Dotted path into the request context. Example: request.path or actor.guild.

op.description: Comparison operator. matches and not_matches take a regex value.

value.description: Right-hand side. Type depends on op.

priority.description: Evaluation priority. Higher wins. The recommended default-deny policy sits at 0; explicit allow rules sit above it.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what the policy does.

rationale.description: Why this policy exists, ideally pointing at a decision memo.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp after which the policy stops matching. Use for temporary exemptions.

owner.description: Person or guild accountable for the policy.
